text:
BOTTOM LINE — Rates stole the tape from AI.
Long yields rose, equities faded, and the day looked like a de-risking pullback after recent 20d gains in QQQ +9.26% and XLK +14.20%. Positioning stayed defensively stretched with SPY PCR_vol 2.04 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.60, while VIX 18.0 sat near its 20d MA 18.1, so this was more repricing than panic.

INDICES — VIX 18.0, flat versus 17.26 headline levels and down 0.0%. Best to worst: DIA -1.08%, SPY -1.20%, QQQ -1.51%, IWM -2.41%; STYLE: LARGE > SMALL by 0.42pp. Biggest cross-asset move: SLV -8.57%, and SPY plus QQQ stayed above their 200-day MAs, so no CTA sell trigger.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 1.0x and QQQ vol_ratio 1.4x, so Nasdaq selling drew more attention than S&P selling. Elevated volume hit SLV 1.9x, GLD 1.5x, IWM 1.4x, XLY 1.4x, and XLK 1.4x; ignored names were PEJ 0.3x, XHE 0.3x, and IBUY 0.1x. QQQ had vol up + price down, a distribution day; SPY was low-conviction down, not accumulation.

SECTORS — XLE Energy +2.36%, XLF Financials -0.37%, XLP Staples -0.40%, XLC Comms -0.88%, XLV Healthcare -1.04%, XLRE Real Estate -1.55%, XLI Industrials -1.78%, XLY Discretionary -1.80%, XLK Technology -1.81%, XLU Utilities -2.29%, XLB Materials -2.65%. Top 2: XLE +2.36% and XLF -0.37%; bottom 2: XLB -2.65% and XLU -2.29%.

THE 7s — Top 5: Freight +3.20% (strongest WAB, weakest FDX), Software +2.36% (strongest SNPS, weakest ORCL), Buildout +2.13% (strongest FIX, weakest GFF), Cloud +1.20% (strongest NOW, weakest AMZN), Insurance +0.89% (strongest TRV, weakest KNSL). Bottom 5: New Staples -0.25% (strongest ELF, weakest COTY), Health -0.25% (strongest MDT, weakest MRK), Defense -0.73% (strongest CMI, weakest TTM), Old Metals -1.60% (strongest FCX, weakest NUE), New Metals -3.62% (strongest MP, weakest NEM). Internal divergence was sharp in New Metals, with MP far better than NEM.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is Jun 17, 2026, with CME hold 100%, cut 0%, hike 0%, and the stance tally is Hawk 6 / Neutral 5 / Dove 4. USD liquidity stress is 38/100, labeled Normal, with Reserve Scarcity 79th percentile as the most-stressed component; ON-RRP was $0.6B and TED spread 0.09% stayed tame.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 2.04 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.60 show heavy protection demand, while SPY skew +8.1% and QQQ skew +10.4% mean puts still carry premium. SPY GEX flip 746.0 and QQQ GEX flip 645.0 sit close enough to spot to matter if pressure persists, and VIX 18.0 was basically at its 20d MA 18.1. MOVE 71.7 was elevated but not stressed.

CROSS-ASSET — USO +0.68% and UNG +1.64% versus GLD -0.76% and SLV -8.57% is a clean inflation-input bid with a precious-metals unwind. That price mix matches the rate story: yields up, real assets mixed, and silver’s -8.57% drop looked like a crowded squeeze.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 53.2/100, neutral. Top movers: SLV -8.57% in cross-asset, MSOS -8.88% in industries, and WGMI -7.05% in industries; 20d best sector XLK +14.20%, worst sector XLU -4.96%, best basket The Semi 7 +40.66%, worst basket The Medtech 7 -11.61%. Vol spikes: SLX 5.9x at -3.13% and CPER 2.5x at -4.70% were distribution, not accumulation.

WATCH — SLV -8.57% needs follow-through check after 1.9x volume and -4.0% skew. QQQ -1.51% matters with PCR_vol 1.60, VXN 24.1, and GEX flip 645.0 near spot. XLE +2.36% deserves attention because it was the only sector higher while XLY and XLK both printed 1.4x volume and negative returns.
